steps.title: Find which form shows the coefficient equal to f(1)
steps.content: A coefficient equals $f(1)$ only when the exponential factor becomes 1 at $x=1$. In Desmos, make a one-row table with $x_1=1$, then evaluate each choice at that input. This quickly shows which coefficient is actually the value of the function at $x=1$: choice A gives $50(2)^2$, choice B gives $80(2)^1$, choice C gives $128(2)^0$, and choice D gives $205(2)^{-1}$. Only choice C has exponent 0 at $x=1$, so only choice C makes the coefficient itself equal to $f(1)$.

steps.title: Evaluate f(1) for choice C
steps.content:
For $f(x) = 128(2)^{x-1}$:
$$f(1) = 128(2)^{1-1} = 128(2)^0 = 128 \cdot 1 = 128$$
The coefficient 128 shown in form C equals $f(1) = k$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
